source: Over the past decade, the cost of solar photovoltaic electricity has fallen by roughly 90%, and onshore wind by about 70%. In many regions new renewable capacity is now cheaper to build than new fossil-fuel plants. Critics note that intermittency still requires storage or backup generation, and that grid upgrades are a significant capital cost.

Remote work expanded sharply after 2020. Proponents argue it widens hiring pools, cuts commuting time, and can raise focus for certain tasks. Skeptics point to weaker spontaneous collaboration, onboarding challenges for junior staff, and blurred work-life boundaries. Survey data is mixed and varies a lot by role and industry.

Large language models can draft, summarize, and translate text at scale. Supporters highlight productivity gains and accessibility. Detractors raise concerns about hallucinated facts, training-data provenance, energy use, and the risk of over-reliance in high-stakes settings such as medicine and law.
